gates.detail: No-cost and dry-run artifacts are diagnostic only.
Requires registry pins, complete import, and every normalized record/probe.
At least one complete registry-verified public source must be represented in the exact run set.
The exact seeded 10,000-message InfiniteBuild protocol must run alongside public evidence.
Recent, learned rolling, raw flat hybrid, and Continuum modes must all run.
Requires at least three complete repetitions of every planned run.
Requires live JobProcessor compilation plus SqliteCandidateIndex/RetrievalEngine metadata on every memory run.
Every Continuum repetition must build claims and a wiki page, and each dataset must select compiled rather than only raw memory at least once.
The rolling baseline must use paid recursive model summaries, never expected answers or an oracle fixture.
Both memory modes require indexed vectors and query embeddings in addition to lexical retrieval.
Every non-deterministic run must be judged by a separately configured model.
Full plus lexical, vector, reranker, temporal, topic-page, and graph removals must exercise exact production RetrievalEngine flags.
Full retrieval must outperform no-graph or no-topic-pages on accuracy, Recall@10, or temporal accuracy; unchanged results remain an honest miss.
The deterministic context-configuration-stratified human sample must be complete with at least 80% judge agreement.
Every controlled and ablation run must finish without an error.
Worker, retrieval, summarization, answer, and judge calls must fit the atomic shared USD 100 plan.
Continuum answer accuracy must improve by at least 15% relative to learned rolling summary.
Continuum Retrieval Recall@10 must be at least 90%.
At least one temporal probe is required and current/superseded accuracy must be at least 90%.
Unsupported personal-memory assertions must remain below 2%.
At least one 10,000-message Continuum checkpoint is required and average selected context must be at least 60% smaller than full replay.
Median end-to-end controlled response latency must be no more than 25% above the highest-accuracy non-graph baseline (fastest wins ties).

claimBoundaries: This artifact compares controlled context strategies; it does not establish superiority to ChatGPT or Codex without separate frozen manual black-box captures.
Interactive first-token and post-turn-searchability claims require a separately validated live latency artifact and are never inferred from this offline runner.
Controlled-mode comparisons hold the lexical fixture reranker constant; the no-reranking ablation isolates only that fixture's removal and is not evidence for the production provider reranker itself.
limitations: A completed independent human audit is attached only after the run; the initial artifact remains ineligible until finalized without new API calls.

Controlled response latency includes synchronous retrieval before answering; learned-summary and memory-compilation maintenance is reported separately and cannot replace the interactive live-latency harness.
Public dataset licenses and registry pins remain separate from Continuum's MIT license.
Provider and model behavior can change even when visible model names are pinned; preserve raw runs and dates.
